final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing styled smartphone photo of fashionable coffee and desserts in a 韓国カフェ aesthetic, with hand-drawn white-pen annotation overlays on every visible element — no people.

Style/medium: needs a "韓国カフェ風 Instagram" aesthetic — references in the spirit of 2010s Korean cafe culture flat-lay photography, Kinfolk magazine intimate tabletop editorial style, Japanese 雑誌スクラップ手書きノート zine tradition; exact stylistic execution is yours
Composition: must include a top-down or slight-angle smartphone-style flat-lay framing; hand-drawn white-pen annotations must be layered over every visible element with arrows or dashed lines guiding the eye between items
Subject: must include fashionable coffee drink(s) and dessert/sweets as focal subjects — no people, no human figures
Scene/backdrop: must include a 韓国カフェ interior tabletop setting, styled and photogenic
Lighting/mood: needs a soft, warm, natural-light café atmosphere — exact light quality is yours
Color palette: needs a muted, warm, slightly desaturated tone consistent with Korean cafe Instagram aesthetics — exact palette is yours
Materials/textures: must include hand-drawn white-pen overlay elements rendered as:
  · thin, single-stroke rough lines as if drawn with a white pen in one continuous motion
  · outline traces around the perimeter of each object
  · arrows and dashed lines creating visual flow between elements
  · decorative accents: steam wisps, sparkle glints, small heart shapes, tiny emoji-like faces — used sparingly with intentional negative space
Text (verbatim): must include handwritten-style Japanese annotation labels (70% hiragana, 30% simple kanji) placed near each element, short diary-tone one-liners:
  · near drink → taste / temperature / mood (e.g., 「すっきり、やさしい甘さ♡」)
  · near food → texture / deliciousness (e.g., 「しっとり、さいこう…」)
  · near space/backdrop → atmosphere (e.g., 「落ち着く、いい時間」)
  · overall caption area → single summary line (e.g., 「しあわせすぎた〜☁️」)
Constraints: annotation overlays must feel hand-drawn and slightly uneven, not digital-perfect; text must remain Japanese handwritten style throughout; negative space must be preserved between annotations
Avoid: people, human hands, overly polished digital vector lines, crowded or cluttered annotation placement
